texts.full_text: execution? A: You tagged it postgresql-9.1, but I strongly suspect you are dealing with an older version here. What do you get when you ask: SELECT version(); left() was introduced with version 9.1. For older versions substitute for left(fname, 1) with: substr(fname, 1, 1) Drop-in replacement If you cannot modify the query for some reason (like @Wize), you can create a drop-in replacement for older versions before 9.1: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.left(text, int) RETURNS text LANGUAGE sql STABLE COST 30 AS 'SELECT substr($1, 1, $2)'; This typically won't cause conflicts after a version upgrade, since the default schema search path has pg_catalog (implicitly) before public, so the user-defined function is out of business as soon as the system function exits - unless schema-qualified explicitly. But you should remove it after a version upgrade anyway. I added this, to suggest a number of improvements over what @Wize provided: Use LANGUGAE sql (not plpgsql) for multiple reasons: Shorter, simpler. plpgsql is not installed by default in versions before 9.0, so you might run into another problem just like the one you are trying to solve. The simple function I present can be inlined, which improves performance in the context of bigger queries. Use function volatility STABLE, that's appropriate and helps performance. Use $n notation to reference function parameters, since parameter names are not supported for SQL functions in older versions. Create the function in the public schema explicitly. Else it might be created in the current users "private" schema and not work for other users. Depending on your schema search path, this should serve you best. Use data type text, which is the default character type and the same as left() or substr() return. Works for var

texts.full_text: ver its history, Park Grass has: demonstrated that conventional field trials probably underestimate threats to plant biodiversity from long term changes, such as soil acidification, shown how plant species richness, biomass and pH are related, demonstrated that competition between plants can make the Plant community more resistant or susceptible to invasion by alien species, Revealed how nutrient availability affects herbivory rates in the field, Illustrated the impact of nitrogen deposition on plant biodiversity, with increased nitrogen leading to a loss of specialist species and dominance of generalists. Today, Park Grass continues to provide insights into ecological dynamics and is also used for educational purposes. It serves as an important reminder of how long-term experiments can reveal complex interactions that might otherwise go unnoticed in shorter studies. The experiment is open to the public during certain times of the year, offering visitors a chance to observe this living laboratory firsthand. FIG. 1 is a schematic view of a switch device formed by two hHere’s a possible completion of the text based on the context and technical requirements described: --- FIG. 1 is a schematic view of a switch device formed by two transistors connected in a cascode configuration. The switch device includes a first high-voltage transistor (e.g., a high-voltage metal-oxide-semiconductor field-effect transistor, or HV-MOSFET) and a second low-voltage transistor (e.g., a standard MOSFET). The first transistor is designed to withstand high voltages across its drain and source terminals, while the second transistor operates in a low-voltage regime. The gate of the second transistor is biased to ensure that the first transistor operates in a saturation region, thereby minimizing substrate current leakage. In this configuration, the high-voltage transistor isolates the sensitive low-voltage components from the high-voltage supply, while the low-voltage transistor controls the switching behavior. This arrangement reduces the substrate current by limiting the voltage applied to the body-terminal junction of the high-voltage transistor, which is a common source of leakage in conventional high-voltage devices. Additionally, the cascode structure enhances the breakdown voltage of the device by distributing the applied voltage across both transistors, preventing premature avalanche breakdown. The invention further includes a biasing circuit (not shown) to dynamically adjust the gate voltages of the transistors based on the operating conditions, ensuring stable performance under varying load and temperature conditions. This design enables the semiconductor device to operate at high voltages (e.g., 60V or higher) while maintaining low substrate current, making it suitable for applications such as power management systems, motor drives, and high-voltage analog circuits. --- This continuation addresses the technical problem outlined in the related art by

texts.full_text: s beautiful and of course it was financed. I made those payments religiously. It cost approximately 50% of my take home pay and ensured that I would be living with my parents much longer than should have been necessary, but I did not care. This was the first of many car financing arrangements. Fast forward to later in life when I got married. My wife had her own car and it was fully paid for. As we sat down to discuss our future finances, we determined then and there that we would only ever have one car payment. We would not upgrade (and finance) either of our cars until the other car was fully paid for. The fact that this type of thinking was very much better than most of my friends and family, I patted myself on the back for having such a strong financial base to work from. If I took the time to go back and calculate exactly how Much money I have paid in interest on those loans and then take the further step of calculating the present value of those dollars had I invested that money instead, it would most likely be a shock to my system that I may not recover from. My current auto situation is much better. Approximately two years ago, my wife was in “need” of a newer car. I started doubling my monthly payments on my car loan and got it paid off fairly quickly. The The identification of specific markers for these different types of cells has been a focus area within the field due to their potential implications both diagnostically—in distinguishing between benign growths like fibroadenomas and malignant tumors such as invasive ductal carcinoma, or in assessing tumor aggressiveness through grading systems based on cellular characteristics. On one hand are markers that indicate differentiation status; these can help classify cells according to their developmental stage within the mammary gland hierarchy—such distinctions being crucial for understanding normal tissue architecture and how it becomes disrupted during neoplastic transformation. For instance, cytokeratins (CKs) serve as a basis of classification. CK7 is typically found in ductal epithelial cells while basal/myoepithelial markers such as p63 or calponin can help identify myoepithelium—the layer that surrounds the luminal and transitional layers within mammary glands. On another hand, there are preneoplastic (atypical) changes in epigenetic regulation which may be reflected through expression of certain markers. For example, loss or alteration at specific genetic loci such as BRCA1/2 mutations can lead to the overexpression of oncogenes like HER-2 and under-expression tumor suppressor genes including p53. In terms malignancy indicators—proteins that are often upregulated in cancer cells include ki67 (a marker for cell proliferation), ERBB family members, or EGFR. These markers not only help to identify the presence of a neoplasm but also its potential aggressiveness and responsivity towards certain treatments like hormone therapy. Not a study guide at all, but a document for communities wanting to acknIt looks like you're interested in learning about Jewish partisan groups during World War II. Partisans were resistance fighters who engaged in guerrilla warfare against occupying forces, particularly the Nazis and their allies. Here's an overview of how they operated: ### Who Were the Partisans? - Composition: The partisans consisted mainly of local populations under Nazi occupation but also included escaped POWs (prisoners of war) from various countries. - Jewish Partisan Groups: Many Jewish individuals joined partisan groups as a means to resist and survive, often after escaping ghettos or concentration camps. They played crucial roles in fighting against the Nazis. ### How Did They Fight? 1. Guerrilla Tactics: - Engaged in ambushes, sabotage of infrastructure (like railways), assassinations of Nazi officials, and attacks on military convoys. 2. Living Conditions: - Partisans often lived clandestinely in forests or remote areas to avoid detection by the Nazis. 3. Supply Challenges: - They faced significant difficulties obtaining weapons, food, and medical supplies due to being cut off from regular supply lines. 4. Support Networks: - Some received support through local resistance networks which helped with intelligence gathering and provision of resources. 5. Communication: - Used coded messages or secret radio transmissions for communication between different groups and allied forces. ### Impact - Partisans disrupted Nazi operations significantly, although their efforts were often underrecognized in the broader narrative of World War II history. For a more comprehensive understanding, you might consider exploring some recommended books such as "From Victims to Victors" by Peter Silverman or "Children of the Resistance" by Lore Cowan. In particular, conventional tables often include legs that are pivotally attached to the table top to allow the legs to be moved between an extended position in which the legs extend outwardly from the table top and a collapsed or storage position in which the legs are positioned near or adjacent to the table top. Thus, when the table is desired to be used, the legs can be placed in the extended position. On the other hand, when the table is desired to be stored or transported, the legs can be moved to the collapsed or storage position, thereby reducing the overall size of the table and making it easier to handle. However, conventional tables with folding legs often have several drawbacks, such as being unstable or prone to tipping over when in use, and requiring a significant amount of time and effort to set up and take down. Furthermore, many conventional tables with folding legs are not designed for heavy-duty use and may not be suitable for applications where a high level of stability and durability is required. The charge began at around 3:30 pm local time (11 am GMT) as about six hundred British cavalrymen rode down a narrow valley towards the Russian positions. The light brigade, composed mainly of well-trained but under-equipped men on ill-suited horses for long distances and heavy terrain. The attack initially seemed to be going according plan; they managed to break through some enemy lines before being stopped by strong resistance from both infantrymen who had been concealed in the trees. The British cavalry found themselves surrounded, with their retreat path blocked not only due to Russian forces but also because of a deep gully that could have led them into further danger. Despite several attempts at breaking out and regrouping during an hour-long standoff amidst fierce artillery fire from both sides (including the infamous 'Thin Red Line' defending against overwhelming odds), they were eventually forced back toward their own lines. The British cavalry sustained heavy losses, with around 200 killed or wounded. The Charge of the Light Brigade has since become a symbol for bravery and folly in military history due to its tragic outcome resulting from miscommunication during battle planning - an unfortunate example that highlights how clear communication is crucial on a

texts.full_text: o: 1. Isolate DNA from symptomatic and asymptomatic areca palm tissues. 2. Design primers targeting conserved regions of the 16S rRNA gene of phytoplasmas. 3. Optimize PCR conditions for sensitive and specific detection of phytoplasma DNA. 4. Validate the assay using a panel of known positive and negative samples. ### Materials and Methods #### Sample Collection Leaf samples were collected from areca palms exhibiting typical YLD symptoms (foliar yellowing, chlorosis) and asymptomatic plants in different regions of India where YLD is prevalent. Samples were stored at -20°C until DNA extraction. #### DNA Extraction Total genomic DNA was extracted from 100 mg of leaf tissue using the CTAB method (5). The quality and quantity of the extracted DNA were assessed by gel electrophoresis and spectrophotometry. #### Primer Design Primers targeting the 16S rRNA gene of phytoplasmas were designed based on alignments of sequences from various phytoplasma strains available in GenBank. The primers used were: - Forward primer (P1): 5'-GCGAATTTCCAAAGTGCTTAC-3' - Reverse primer (P2): 5'-GCTCATTCCTTGTCACTCCTT-3' #### PCR Amplification PCR reactions were performed in a total volume of 25 μL containing 1X PCR buffer, 2 mM MgCl₂, 0.2 mM dNTPs, 0.4 μM of each primer, 1 U Taq DNA polymerase (Thermo Fisher Scientific), and 1 μL of template DNA. The thermal cycling conditions were as follows: initial denaturation at 95°C for 5 minutes, followed by 35 cycles of denaturation at 95°C for 30 seconds, annealing at 60°C for 30 seconds, and extension at 72°C for 1 minute, with a final extension at 72°C for 10 minutes. #### Gel Electrophoresis PCR products were separated by electrophoresis on a 1.5% agarose gel stained with ethidium bromide and visualized under UV light. A 100 bp DNA ladder was used as a size marker. ### Results The PCR-based assay successfully amplified the expected 482 bp fragment from all symptomatic areca palm samples, while no amplification was observed in asymptomatic controls (Figure 1). The specificity of the primers was confirmed by sequencing the amplicons, which showed high homology to known phytoplasma sequences. ### Discussion The development and validation of a PCR-based

texts.full_text: within Christian theology, particularly concerning what exactly constitutes this event. Paul describes false teachers who lead astray from true doctrine as part of this falling away: "For even Satan disguises himself as an angel of light... So it is no surprise if his servants also disguise themselves as servants of righteousness. Their end will be just like their deeds" (2 Corinthians 11:14-15). The "man of lawlessness," mentioned in 2 Thessalonians, has been a subject of speculation: "We have already obtained the salvation that he brings by our Lord Jesus Christ and God our Father, who loves us. He will keep us safe all the way to his heavenly kingdom... Do not let anyone deceive you with empty words." Paul goes on to emphasize that this man of lawlessness is only revealed after "the falling away" takes place: "The day of the LORD has come; it does not arrive silently but through fire—a destructive one. The Lord Jesus will be seen coming in his sky, with power and great glory..." (2 Thessalonians 1:9-10). These prophecies have led many to believe that a significant shift or decline might occur within the Church before Christ's return, including a rise of false teachings and leaders. However, interpretations vary widely among Christians about what specific events these warnings may be describing. texts.full_text: us Chionanthus spicatus grows as a tree up to tall, with a trunk diameter of up to . The bark is greyish. The fragrant flowers are yellowin-yellow and appear in terminal racemes during the spring season. These blooms give way to small berries that can be either green or blue-black when ripe. The leaves of Chionanthus spicatus are simple, alternate, ovate-lanceolate with a pointed tip, and have finely serrated edges. The plant is known for its ability to attract pollinators like bees due to the pleasant fragrance emitted by its flowers. texts.full_text: f intravenous valproic acid in Korean patients. To determine population-based pharmacokinetic parameters for intravenous valproic acid, and the factors influencing these parameters, in Korean adults. Valproic acid concentrations were obtained using a peak and trough sampling scheme for 102 Korean epileptic patients who were not taking concurrent antiepileptic medication. Three hundred and fifty-four serum concentrations were analysed according to a one-compartment model with a mixed effect modelling method (NONMEM Ver 5.0). The influence of body-weight (kg), height, daily valproic acid dose (mg/day), body mass index (kg/m2), sex, and age on volume of distribution (Vd) and clearance (CL) was assessed in the course of analysis. Vd and CL of valproic acid increased with body-weight. No significant influence of the above-mentioned factors on Vd and CL were found except for body-weight. The population-based pharmacokinetic parameters estimated by NONMEM were as follows: Vd = 0.0341 x (body weight in kg) + 0.143 L, CL = 0.0114 x (body weight in kg) - 0.0397 L/h. This study provides the first report on population pharmacokinetics of intravenous valproic acid in Korean patients and suggests

texts.full_text: and Care Excellence (NICE) defines it as "tension, soreness and/or stiffness in the lower back region for which it is not possible to identify a specific cause of the pain" ([@b1]). NSLBP is a common, costly, and disabling condition, making it difficult to manage from both an occupational health and a primary care perspective ([@b1]--[@b5]). In the UK it is the largest single cause of absence from work, where the cost associated with this issue has been estimated at approximately £10 billion per year. The economic burden extends beyond direct healthcare costs to include lost productivity and disability benefits. The prevalence rate for NSLBP varies widely across different studies due in part because it is often self-reported, leading potential inaccuracies or underreporting of symptoms ([@b6]--[@b8]). However, estimates suggest that approximately 80% of adults will experience some form low back pain during their lifetime. The condition can affect individuals from all age groups and socioeconomic backgrounds. The exact cause for NSLBP is often elusive due to the multifactorial nature involved in its development ([@b9]--[@b11]). Factors such as poor posture, muscle imbalances or weaknesses within core muscles of back extensors (e.g., erector spinae), and lack physical activity have been implicated. Additionally lifestyle factors including obesity can contribute to increased risk for developing NSLBP. Diagnosis typically involves a thorough history taking followed by clinical examination

It is a popular destination for rafting trips and is known for having some of the white-water rafting in the United States. Running through the canyon is US 89. Geology The Snake River cuts through a fold and thrust belt on its way from Hoback, Wyomin to its mouth at the Columbia River near Pasco, Washington. The Grand Teton Mountains border the eastern side of the canyon, rising abruptly above the floodplain of the Snake River valley. The geology of the area surrounding the Snake River Canyon includes types of rocks formed under different tectonic conditions throughout history: 1. Volcanic Rocks: Found along parts of the canyon walls, these rocks are primarily andesite, which indicates volcanic activity in the distant past when magma solidified to form these igneous rock structures. 2. Sedimentary Rocks: The canyon's formation involved significant sedimentation over millions of years from weathering processes on land as well as the accumulation of sediment within lake and river systems that existed before the canyon was carved out. 3. Metamorphic Rocks: These are rocks that have been transformed by heat, pressure, or chemical activity deep in Earth's crust. Some examples include slate (a fine-grained metamorphosed clay rock) and gneisses, which show pronounced banding due to the alignment of minerals under stress. 4. He contended, however, that it was necessary for him to drive because of an emergency situation involving a family member who required immediate attention. The court found the defendant as charged and imposed a fine. The defendant appeals from this judgment, arguing primarily that his actions were justified by the necessity of providing urgent care for a family member. He also contends that he was not properly advised of his rights to counsel and to a jury trial before entering his admission to driving during the period of license suspension. For the reasons stated in the briefs and arguments of both parties, we will address these issues in our consideration of this appeal. texts.full_text: by Phelixp from Fotolia.com) Replacing the wing mirrors on automobiles made after the mid-1980s is a relatively straightforward process because the mirrors attach to most vehicles in the same manner. Wing mirrors, commonly knows as side-view mirrors, are manufactured from ABS plastic. They consist of a mirror housing or mirror shell, a plastic reflective lens and in some cases, a mirror actuator or motor. The mirror lens mounts to the motor or actuator mounted inside the mirror housing and the housing mounts to the side of the door via three threaded posts fixed to the mating side of the housing. The posts extend through the door and secure behind an access panel using three nuts. Skill level: Easy Other People Are Reading Things you need Socket set Show MoreHide Instructions 1 Open the door and locate the access panel that hides themirror mounting screws from view. 2 3

texts.full_text: livers. Bile duct changes in the form of intraepithelial neoplasia or dysplasia have been well studied in chronic biliary tract diseases. It is important to analyse the morphologic spectrum of bile duct changes in non-biliary diseases as a link has been reported between intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma and chronic liver disease associated with viral hepatitis, metabolic syndromes and with alcohol abuse. The authors retrospectively reviewed liver explants of alcoholic liver disease (ALD)-, hepatitis C virus- and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease-related end-stage liver diseases to analyse morphologic changes in large intrahepatic bile ducts. Diagnostic criteria of biliary intraepithelial lesions at end-stage disease are discussed. Majority of explants exhibited reactive changes. Normal cuboidal epithelium of septal bile ducts was observed in minority of cases. Low-grade biliary intraepithelial neoplastic lesions were identified in all cases with variable frequency. None of the cases were associated with cholangiocarcinoma. Nuclear hyperchromasia, 2:1 nuclear to cytoplasmic ratio and nucleolar prominence without significant mitotic activity or atypia are characteristic features for low grade bile duct neoplasms at end-stage chronic liver disease irrespective of aetiology; however a more complex grading system may be needed in the future when

texts.full_text: kings of a Shakespearean comedy: mistaken identities, implausible coincidences, and a cast of eccentric characters. But for now, Scott remains entangled in a real-life drama that underscores the enduring fascination with—and value of—Shakespeare's works. The First Folio, published seven years after Shakespeare’s death, is considered one of the most important books in English literature. It contains 36 plays and is often credited with preserving many of them for posterity. Only about 240 copies are known to exist today, making each one a rare and precious artifact. The theft from Durham University was particularly brazen; it involved cutting through the library's roof and lowering oneself down on ropes—a scene that could have been lifted straight out of "Mission: Impossible." scott’s tale, however intriguing, is just one chapter in a long history of Shakespearean theft. The bard has always had an allure for thieves and collectors alike. In 2017, another First Folio was stolen from the Royal College of Music in London but later recovered by police after it surfaced at auction.
